Jami' al-'Adiliyya - Procedural Model, Ontology Graph & Live Dashboard

-
Drag to scrub day-by-day through the real 2010–2023 station record. Labeled ticks are documented events - click one, or any node/row elsewhere on the page, to jump straight to it.
Procedural Model (grayscale) - click a zonenone selected
Opens on the mosque site, not the district - at true district scale the model and real relief are close to sub-pixel. The mosque model is the darkest mass on the map, embedded as a real 3D layer directly in the map's own scene (MapLibre GL custom layer, one shared WebGL context) - not a separate overlay - sitting at its true geo-coordinate at every zoom/tilt level. Around it: pale spikes are real district topography (ASTER 30m, OpenTopoData, exaggerated 12x so it stays visible - see footer), light-gray blocks are generic building massing over the real UNOSAT neighborhood footprints, and the neighborhoods themselves sink into voids below ground scaled to real % buildings damaged - loss shown as absence, not presence. Drag to orbit/tilt the whole scene · scroll to zoom · click a building part (or a zone button, or a zone node in the ontology graph) to select it - the selected part glows white and the view zooms in; deselect to zoom back out · "Zoom to Aleppo district" for the wide view. The model itself is now the user's own Rhino model (minaret, central dome, portico, plus the remaining unlabeled layers grouped as "structure"), simplified for web performance from ~474,000 real vertices to ~21,000 (see footer) - not the earlier DXF-procedural approximation. The Rhino model's minaret measures ~35.7m; the damage report separately documents its shaft height as 41m - both are real figures from different real sources and are shown as such, not reconciled into one number.
Ontology - Force-Directed Graph (populates with timeline)
